title: Make plan approvals visible and queue-safe
summary: EVE now watches for Plan requests as soon as the macOS utility launches, raises a floating approval window for new arrivals, preserves in-progress reviews when additional agents declare plans, and presents a multi-request queue with inline editing. The web overview polls for pending approvals, displays a prominent multi-plan banner, and renders only the complete recent-repository cards that fit the available width.
userVisibleChange: New Plan requests proactively surface in a review window instead of waiting behind the menu-bar icon. Reviewers can switch among multiple requests, edit in place without losing context, and see pending work on the web overview; repository cards no longer clip at the viewport edge.

risks.title: Approval review UI remains concentrated in one Swift source file
risks.mitigation: The review found no functional or standards violation; window infrastructure and review views can be split in a future maintenance refactor if they evolve independently.

timeline.title: Locked UX queue Plan revision 1
timeline.summary: Declared popup attention, inline editing, multi-plan handling, pending web banner, and responsive repository-card acceptance criteria.

timeline.title: Implemented proactive native queue and responsive web surfaces
timeline.summary: Moved queue watching to app startup, added one-time new-request attention, built master-detail review and inline editing, added pending banner polling, and measured card capacity.

timeline.title: Ran native, web, Go, and packaging verification
timeline.summary: All targeted and repository-wide tests and builds passed; Playwright confirmed four complete cards at 2048px and five at 2400px.

timeline.title: Completed standards and spec reviews
timeline.summary: The review found and prompted a fix for new arrivals stealing selection during inline edits. Re-review found no remaining spec gaps or hard standards violations.

decisions.title: Raise a floating local review panel for new requests
decisions.rationale: MenuBarExtra has no supported programmatic open API. A reusable NSPanel provides reliable visible attention while preserving the trusted-local, no-Dock utility model.

decisions.title: Preserve active selection when another plan arrives
decisions.rationale: New arrivals should raise attention without switching away from an in-progress inline edit and discarding the reviewer’s draft.

decisions.title: Measure repository-card capacity
decisions.rationale: ResizeObserver computes how many full minimum-width cards fit, allowing larger screens to show more while preventing partial overflow cards.

validation.output: TypeScript checking and Vite production build passed; embedded UI assets regenerated.
macOS app and test target built; all 9 tests passed.
npm package dry-run succeeded with 5 files.

artifacts.description: Web overview at 2048px showing exactly four complete recent-repository cards with no clipped fifth card.
